infobox.value: Stephen L. Schultz ([D](/wiki/United_States_Democratic_Party "United States Democratic Party")) First elected 2017 [Town council](/wiki/Town_council "Town council")   * Rick Page ([R](/wiki/United_States_Republican_Party "United States Republican Party")) * Joseph Bellanca, Jr. ([R](/wiki/United_States_Republican_Party "United States Republican Party")) * Robert Barley, Jr. ([D](/wiki/United_States_Democratic_Party "United States Democratic Party")) * Michael Stafford ([D](/wiki/United_States_Democratic_Party "United States Democratic Party"))

* 14467 (Henrietta) * 14586 ([West Henrietta](/wiki/West_Henrietta,_New_York "West Henrietta, New York")) * 14534 ([Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford_(village),_New_York "Pittsford (village), New York")) * 14543 ([Rush](/wiki/Rush,_New_York "Rush, New York")) * 14618, 14623 ([Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"))

is a [town](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Town "Administrative divisions of New York") in [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States, and a suburb of the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"). The population of Henrietta was 47,096 as of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), up from 42,581 in [2010](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census"). Henrietta is home to the [Rochester Institute of Technology](/wiki/Rochester_Institute_of_Technology "Rochester Institute of Technology"), the National Technical Institute for the Deaf and one of the largest retail shopping districts in Monroe County.

**Henrietta** is a [town](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_New_York#Town "Administrative divisions of New York") in [Monroe County](/wiki/Monroe_County,_New_York "Monroe County, New York"), [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"), United States, and a suburb of the city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_New_York "Rochester, New York"). The population of Henrietta was 47,096 as of the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census"), up from 42,581 in [2010](/wiki/2010_United_States_census "2010 United States census"). Henrietta is home to the [Rochester Institute of Technology](/wiki/Rochester_Institute_of_Technology "Rochester Institute of Technology"), the National Technical Institute for the Deaf and one of the largest retail shopping districts in Monroe County.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/71/James_Tinker_House_%E2%80%94_Henrietta%2C_Monroe_County%2C_New_York.jpg/330px-James_Tinker_House_%E2%80%94_Henrietta%2C_Monroe_County%2C_New_York.jpg)](/wiki/File:James_Tinker_House_%E2%80%94_Henrietta,_Monroe_County,_New_York.jpg)

Tinker Cobblestone Homestead (2022)

The first residents of present-day were [Native Americans](/wiki/Indigenous_peoples_of_the_Americas "Indigenous peoples of the Americas"). Although no evidence of Native American villages has been found in Henrietta, numerous artifacts and skeletons have been unearthed by farmers and [archeologists](/wiki/Archeologist "Archeologist") over the past 200 years.[[4]](#cite_note-4) With the end of the [American Revolution](/wiki/American_Revolution "American Revolution"), several [Iroquois](/wiki/Iroquois "Iroquois") tribes that had sided with the British were forced to leave [Upstate New York](/wiki/Upstate_New_York "Upstate New York"). As a result, land became available for European settlers in the [Finger Lakes](/wiki/Finger_Lakes "Finger Lakes") and [Genesee Valley](/wiki/Genesee_Valley "Genesee Valley") regions.

The town of Henrietta was named after [Henrietta Laura Pulteney, Countess of Bath](/wiki/Laura_Pulteney,_1st_Countess_of_Bath "Laura Pulteney, 1st Countess of Bath") in Great Britain.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Her father [Sir William Pulteney, 5th Baronet](/wiki/Sir_William_Pulteney,_5th_Baronet "Sir William Pulteney, 5th Baronet"), a major British investor from [the Pulteney Association](/wiki/The_Pulteney_Association "The Pulteney Association"), owned the land that became the town. Henrietta Pulteney never visited the United states. Pulteney eventually sold his holdings, which then passed through the hands of several American investors.[[6]](#cite_note-Elam_book-6)

The first European settlers in Henrietta were Major Ezekiel Scott and his wife Catherine, who purchased 900 acres (360 ha) in 1790. Most of the original arriving settlers were English.[[6]](#cite_note-Elam_book-6) Two villages, East and West Henrietta, arose in the area, both part of the town of [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford,_New_York "Pittsford, New York"). In 1818, a dispute about the recording of deeds prompted residents of the two villages to secede from Pittsford and form the town of Henrietta.[[6]](#cite_note-Elam_book-6)

With the opening of the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal") in 1825, Henrietta farmers gained access to the New York City market for their crops and the town prospered. In 1826, the [Monroe Academy](/wiki/Monroe_Academy "Monroe Academy") opened in Henrietta, becoming the first incorporated school in Monroe County.

After the end of [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), the population of Henrietta rose from 3,000 to approximately 14,000 in ten years as suburban development grew around the city of Rochester. The opening of the [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway") through Henrietta in 1954 made the town more desirable for commercial development. In 1968, the [Rochester Institute of Technology](/wiki/Rochester_Institute_of_Technology "Rochester Institute of Technology") (RIT) moved from Rochester to a new campus by the Genesee River in Henrietta. That same year, the [National Technical Institute for the Deaf](/wiki/National_Technical_Institute_for_the_Deaf "National Technical Institute for the Deaf") (NTID) opened next to RIT.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In the 1980s, with the completion of [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") and the building of [the Marketplace Mall](/wiki/The_Marketplace_Mall "The Marketplace Mall"), Henrietta was developed into one of the major retail shopping destinations in Monroe County.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Henrietta has the following listings on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places"):

* [Andrew Short House](/wiki/Andrew_Short_House "Andrew Short House")
* [Antoinette Louisa Brown Blackwell Childhood Home](/wiki/Antoinette_Louisa_Brown_Blackwell_Childhood_Home "Antoinette Louisa Brown Blackwell Childhood Home")
* [Tinker Cobblestone Farmstead](/wiki/Tinker_Cobblestone_Farmstead "Tinker Cobblestone Farmstead").[[9]](#cite_note-nris-9)[[10]](#cite_note-nps-10)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Henrietta is in southern Monroe County and is bounded by the following communities:

* The western border of Henrietta is the [Genesee River](/wiki/Genesee_River "Genesee River"). The west side of the river has the towns of [Chili](/wiki/Chili,_New_York "Chili, New York") and [Wheatland](/wiki/Wheatland,_New_York "Wheatland, New York").
* To the north of Henrietta is the town of [Brighton](/wiki/Brighton,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Brighton, Monroe County, New York"). The city of [Rochester](/wiki/Rochester,_Monroe_County,_New_York "Rochester, Monroe County, New York") is north of Henrietta, but does not share a border with the town. Downtown Rochester is 7 miles (11 km) north of the center of Henrietta.
* To the east are the towns of [Pittsford](/wiki/Pittsford,_New_York "Pittsford, New York") and [Mendon](/wiki/Mendon,_New_York "Mendon, New York").
* To the south is the town of [Rush](/wiki/Rush,_New_York "Rush, New York").

According to the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/U.S._Census_Bureau "U.S. Census Bureau"), Henrietta has a total area of 35.6 square miles (92 km2). Of this total, 35.3 square miles (91 km2) are land and 0.3 square miles (0.78 km2), or 0.83%, are water.[[2]](#cite_note-CenPopGazetteer2024-2) Most of the town drains westward to the Genesee River, but the northeast part of the town drains northeasterly toward [Irondequoit Creek](/wiki/Irondequoit_Creek "Irondequoit Creek"). Both streams are north-flowing tributaries of [Lake Ontario](/wiki/Lake_Ontario "Lake Ontario").

The New York State Thruway ([Interstate 90](/wiki/Interstate_90_in_New_York "Interstate 90 in New York")) and [Interstate 390](/wiki/Interstate_390 "Interstate 390") run through Henrietta. A short section of the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal") passes through the northeastern side of the town.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

## Government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Government")]

Henrietta's town government consists of a [town supervisor](/wiki/Town_supervisor "Town supervisor") and four town board members.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

Other boards and commissions in Henrietta include the [zoning board](/wiki/Zoning_board "Zoning board"), [planning board](/wiki/Urban_planning "Urban planning"), [conservation](/wiki/Conservation_biology "Conservation biology") board, youth board, [recreation commission](/wiki/Recreation "Recreation"), historic site committee, [assessment board](/wiki/Tax_assessment "Tax assessment"), and [library board](/wiki/Public_library "Public library").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of 2020, the population of Henrietta was 47,096.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

As of the 2005–2009 [American Community Survey](/wiki/American_Community_Survey "American Community Survey"), the racial makeup of the town was 81.5% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 7.9% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 0.27% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 5.9% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), and 3.1% [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") and 1.4% other.

| U.S. Decennial Census[[3]](#cite_note-Census_2020-3) | | | |

The [2000 US census](/wiki/2000_United_States_census "2000 United States census") showed a population in Henrietta of 39,028 with 12,823 [households](/wiki/Household "Household") and 8,501 families. The population density was 1,102.2 inhabitants per square mile (425.6/km2). There were 13,243 [housing units](/wiki/Housing_unit "Housing unit") at an average density of 374.0 per square mile (144.4/km2). The racial makeup of the town was 84.3% [white](/wiki/White_(U.S._census) "White (U.S. census)"), 6.9% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._census) "African American (U.S. census)"), .27% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._census) "Native American (U.S. census)"), 5.5% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._census) "Asian (U.S. census)"), .03% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. census)"), .97% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_census) "Race (United States census)"), and 17.3% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 3.03% of the population.

There were 12,823 households, out of which 30.6% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 53.8% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 9.2% had a female householder with no husband present, and 33.7% were non-families. 22.7% of all households were made up of individuals, and 6.9% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.60 and the average family size was 3.09.

In the town, the population was spread out, with 20.4% under the age of 18, 23.9% from 18 to 24, 26.8% from 25 to 44, 19.6% from 45 to 64, and 9.3% who were 65 years of age or older. The [median age](/wiki/Median_age "Median age") was 30 years. For every 100 females, there were 111.7 males. For every 100 females age 18 and over, there were 113.3 males.

The [median income](/wiki/Median_income "Median income") for a household in Henrietta was $51,081, and the median income for a family was $60,803. Males had a median income of $39,636 versus $30,271 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the town was $19,821. About 3.2% of families and 9.1% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 6.3% of those under age 18 and 3.3% of those age 65 or over.

## Communities in Henrietta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Communities in Henrietta")]

### East Henrietta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: East Henrietta")]

The East Henrietta hamlet is located at the crossroads of East Henrietta ([New York State Route 15A](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15A "New York State Route 15A")) and Lehigh Station roads ([New York State Route 253](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_253 "New York State Route 253")). It was the site of the Monroe Academy, the first secondary school in the county, which later became [Rush-Henrietta Senior High School](/wiki/Rush-Henrietta_Senior_High_School "Rush-Henrietta Senior High School").[[14]](#cite_note-14) Also located at this intersection was the [Elihu Kirby House](/wiki/Elihu_Kirby_House "Elihu Kirby House") (1840), a nationally recognized example of [Greek Revival](/wiki/Greek_Revival "Greek Revival") architecture.[[15]](#cite_note-15) It has since been relocated to a residential neighborhood near Stone Road in Henrietta.

### West Henrietta

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: West Henrietta")]

The West Henrietta hamlet is located at the crossroads of West Henrietta ([New York State Route 15](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_15 "New York State Route 15")) and Erie Station roads. This hamlet has several historic buildings:

* West Henrietta Baptist Church, constructed in 1838[[16]](#cite_note-:0-16)
* West Henrietta US Postal Service Office/West Henrietta Fire Department building, built in the 1920s as a school house[[16]](#cite_note-:0-16)
* Former general store building, built in 1906 and now occupied by a pizza restaurant and apartments[[16]](#cite_note-:0-16)
* Former Cartwright Inn building, built as a [tavern](/wiki/Tavern "Tavern") in 1831. Was a restaurant from 1949 until 2007[[17]](#cite_note-17)

### Riverton

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Riverton")]

[Riverton](/wiki/Riverton,_New_York "Riverton, New York") was established in 1973 by the federal government as a [planned community](/wiki/Planned_community "Planned community").

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Education")]

### Universities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Universities")]

* [Rochester Institute of Technology](/wiki/Rochester_Institute_of_Technology "Rochester Institute of Technology") (RIT)
* [National Technical Institute for the Deaf](/wiki/National_Technical_Institute_for_the_Deaf "National Technical Institute for the Deaf") (NTID)

### Schools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Schools")]

* [Bryant & Stratton College](/wiki/Bryant_%26_Stratton_College "Bryant & Stratton College")[[18]](#cite_note-18)
* [Rush-Henrietta Central School District](/wiki/Rush-Henrietta_Central_School_District "Rush-Henrietta Central School District")
* School of the Holy Childhood – a [non-profit](/wiki/Non-profit "Non-profit") agency for people with [developmental disabilities](/wiki/Developmental_disabilities "Developmental disabilities")
* Norman Howard School – a day school for children with disabilities[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* Continental School of Beauty[[20]](#cite_note-20)
* Aab Cardiovascular Research Institute (part of the [University of Rochester](/wiki/University_of_Rochester "University of Rochester"))[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## Notable people

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Henrietta,_New_York&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Notable people")]

* [Joseph L. Biehler](/wiki/Joseph_L._Biehler "Joseph L. Biehler"), U.S. Army major general[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-Wedding-23)[[24]](#cite_note-Accolade-24)
* [John Birdsell](/wiki/John_Birdsell "John Birdsell"), inventor of the [Birdsell Clover Huller](/wiki/Birdsell_Clover_Huller "Birdsell Clover Huller") and founder of the Birdsell Manufacturing Company[[25]](#cite_note-25)
* [Antoinette Brown Blackwell](/wiki/Antoinette_Brown_Blackwell "Antoinette Brown Blackwell"), first ordained female minister in the United States
* [Shenise Johnson](/wiki/Shenise_Johnson "Shenise Johnson"), a former [WNBA](/wiki/WNBA "WNBA") basketball player[[26]](#cite_note-26)
* [Samantha Watson](/wiki/Samantha_Watson "Samantha Watson"), middle-distance runner[[27]](#cite_note-27)
